sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.3 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: A group$G$is called of type$(FP)_{$} if the trivial$G$-module${ Z}$has a resolution by finitely generated projective$G$-modules. The class of all groups of type$(FP)_{ }$has a couple of excellent closure properties with respect to extensions and amalgamated products (R.\,Bieri, Homological dimension of discrete groups, Queen Mary College Math. Notes, London, 1976). Is every periodic group of type$...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
